title: Kudremukh National Park
tagline: Kudremukh National Park — wildlife reserve of Karnataka

overview.short: Kudremukh National Park is a protected area in Chikmagalur and Dakshina Kannada districts of Indian state of Karnataka.
overview.description: Kudremukh National Park is a protected area in Chikmagalur and Dakshina Kannada districts of Indian state of Karnataka. It is named after the mountain in Chikmagalur district, in Karnataka, India. The name literally means 'horse-face' and refers to one picturesque view of a side of the mountain. The name also refers to the small town near the mountain, about 48 kilometres from Karkala and about 20 kilometres from Kalsa.

topPlaces.name: Gangamoola
topPlaces.description: Gangamoola is a hill in the Chikkamagaluru district of the state of Karnataka, India. Also known as Varaha Parvata, it is one of the hills in the Western Ghats range and is known for being the source of three rivers, Tunga, Bhadra and Netravathi.

topPlaces.name: Hanumana Gundi Falls
topPlaces.description: Hanumana Gundi Falls, also known as Suthanabbe Falls or Soothanabbi Falls, is located in the hilly surroundings of the Kudremukh National Park in the Chikkamagaluru district of Karnataka, India. Hanumanagundi Falls is located between Karkala and Lakya Dam in the Kudremukh national park.

items.note: Gangamoola is a hill in the Chikkamagaluru district of the state of Karnataka, India. Also known as Varaha Parvata, it is one of the hills in the Western Ghats range and is known for being the source of three rivers, Tunga, Bhadra and Netravathi.

Hanumana Gundi Falls, also known as Suthanabbe Falls or Soothanabbi Falls, is located in the hilly surroundings of the Kudremukh National Park in the Chikkamagaluru district of Karnataka, India. Hanumanagundi Falls is located between Karkala and Lakya Dam in the Kudremukh national park.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kudremukh National Park?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kudremukh National Park is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kudremukh National Park?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Mangaluru International Airport (46 km) and the nearest railway station is Mangaluru Central (58 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kudremukh National Park?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kudremukh National Park at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kudremukh National Park?
faq.a: Stays in Kudremukh National Park start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kudremukh National Park?
faq.a: The top attractions are Gangamoola, Hanumana Gundi Falls.
